alerts.title: US and Iran agree to "stand down" after multi-day strike exchange; ceasefire fragile
alerts.summary: After 3 days of reciprocal strikes (US airstrikes on Iranian targets, Iranian attacks on Bahrain/Kuwait military facilities, cargo ship strikes in Strait of Hormuz), Washington and Tehran announced mutual de-escalation. However, Iran's foreign ministry maintains threat of "complete halt" to negotiations if US military withdrawal demands unmet. Multiple sources (BBC, CBS News, AP News, CENTCOM) confirm temporary pause but acknowledge structural diplomatic collapse remains unresolved.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Bahrain, Kuwait

alerts.title: Iran sustains 60% U-235 enrichment amid active military escalation
alerts.summary: IAEA confirms Iran maintains 60% uranium enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window estimated. Nuclear chief states inspector access contingent on final deal completion—condition Iran rejects. Concurrent kinetic strike cycle and diplomatic framework deterioration leave minimal de-escalation pathway. Global threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10).

alerts.title: Israel expands strikes into southern Syria as Hezbollah tensions escalate
alerts.summary: IDF confirmed engagement with unidentified hostile forces in southern Syria (34.77°N, 37.93°E) and conducted helicopter strikes. Al Jazeera reports new Israeli assault forcing families to flee. Marks escalation beyond Lebanon border into Syrian territory, indicating broader regional proxy expansion parallel to Iran-US strike cycle.

alerts.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visits Baghdad to discuss Strait security framework
alerts.summary: Rybar reports (~322K views) that Iran's FM traveled to Baghdad for negotiations on Persian Gulf security architecture. Discussions revealed Iran's demand that Hormuz Strait return to full Iranian control and regional security structure be rewritten without foreign interference—hardening demand that contradicts current US-Iran negotiations. Signals Iran escalating maximalist position amid military operations.

alerts.title: US domestic sentiment tilts toward Iran amid judicial/regulatory focus; regional coverage recedes
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows US positive sentiment at 54% with dominant word clusters on Trump, Supreme Court, and regulatory decisions ("court," "firing," "fed"). Iran mentions drop to only 9 occurrences. Indicates US media/public attention pivot away from Middle East conflict toward domestic politics, potentially reducing political pressure on administration to sustain current escalation posture.

analysis_note: The US-Iran strike cycle has achieved temporary pause, but Iran's nuclear program continues advancing toward breakout while Tehran hardens negotiating demands and expands proxy operations into Syria; diplomatic off-ramps remain closed.
